preamble: The phonetic transformation from the open-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ/ to the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ involves a complex series of articulatory adjustments. This shift typically includes a raising of the tongue body, a backing from a front to a back position, and the addition of lip rounding. Such a transformation can manifest as a regular historical sound change, often conditioned by surrounding phonological environments, or as a consistent feature distinguishing dialects of a single language.
phoneticEffects: Raising, Backing, Rounding, sometimes conditioned by assimilation

examples.note: Occurred before a syllable containing */o(ː)/, demonstrating assimilation of rounding and backness.
Observed as a dialectal variation between the Toya dialect and the Northern Aikmel dialect. Specific word examples were not provided in the source.

sources: Berman, H. (2006). Studies in Blackfoot Prehistory. International Journal of American Linguistics, 72(2), 264–284.

Irfana, M., & Himawana, Y. H. (2018). Sasaknese Language of Toya Dialect and Sasaknese Language of Northern Aikmal Dialect. HICE 2018 Their Stories Our Stories and My Story A Portfolio for Teaching Reading and Writing of Personal Narrative.
